# GravitonKV

The first public reproducible KV-cache quantization tradeoff study on AWS Graviton4 CPU with PMU-level mechanism analysis.

**Status: full sweep in progress.** A five-config, six-model, 2k to 32k context matrix on c8g (Graviton4) is being collected. Everything below the pilot section will be regenerated by script from `results/canonical.json` when the sweep lands. No benchmark number in this repository is hand-typed.

## What this measures

llama.cpp exposes independent K-cache and V-cache quantization (`--cache-type-k` / `--cache-type-v`). This study measures what those settings actually cost and buy on Graviton4 (Neoverse V2) CPU, across five KV configurations (f16/f16, q8_0/q8_0, q4_0/q4_0, q8_0/q4_0, q4_0/q8_0), six permissively licensed models, and a 2k/8k/16k/32k context ladder, with prefill throughput, decode throughput, peak memory, retrieval quality (NIAH, RULER-lite subset, perplexity), and PMU counter evidence for the mechanism.

## Pilot finding (July 4, 2026)

From the committed pilot run in [`results/pilot/RESULTS.md`](results/pilot/RESULTS.md) (m8g.xlarge, 4 vCPU Graviton4, relative deltas valid, absolute numbers to be re-baselined on c8g):

On Graviton4 CPU with flash attention, KV-cache quantization is a three-way asymmetric trade versus f16 at 8k context: prefill throughput roughly doubles, decode throughput drops roughly a quarter, peak memory drops 9 to 14 percent, and needle retrieval stays 10/10 for every KV type. GPU prior art at comparable context shows neither speed effect. The decode regression is a headline result here, not a footnote.

## Repository layout

```
harness/     config-driven sweep harness (bash + Python), canonical results schema
results/     canonical results JSON (single source of truth) + pilot artifacts
dashboard/   static Next.js dashboard (Vercel)
mcp/         read-only results MCP server (stdio + remote)
scripts/     derived-view generators, validation, provisioning
docs/        methodology notes
```

## Reproducibility contract

- llama.cpp pinned at commit `2d973636e292ee6f75fadcf08d29cb33511f509f`, built with `-DGGML_CPU_KLEIDIAI=ON`. KleidiAI is reported as the baseline it is, not as our optimization.
- Flash attention (`-fa on`) on every run, including f16 baselines (quantized V cache requires it; comparisons stay apples-to-apples).
- N=10 repetitions where a cell costs under 10 minutes, N=5 minimum everywhere, first rep discarded as warmup, median plus stdev plus CV reported, seed 42, model SHA256s recorded.
- Measured runs on on-demand Graviton4 instances only. CI runs on GitHub's arm64 runners (Azure Cobalt 100, Neoverse N2): CI validates the harness and never produces findings.
- Full environment capture (instance type, CPU, kernel, commit, build flags, library versions, seeds, SHA256s) in every result file.
